cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out this problem step by step. So, Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. First, I need to determine what date that actually is. February typically has 28 days, except in leap years when it has 29. But the question says the last day of February, so regardless of whether it's a leap year or not, the last day would be February 28th. Wait, but if 2001 is a leap year, then it would have February 29th as the last day. Hmm, but 2001 isn't a leap year. Leap years are divisible by 4, so 2000 was a leap year, 2004 would be the next one. So 2001 isn't a leap year. Therefore, Jane was born on February 28, 2001. Now, today is her 16-year-old birthday. That means today's date is February 28, 2017. Wait, because 2001 plus 16 years would be 2017. But wait, let me check that again. If she was born in 2001, then her 16th birthday would be in 2001 + 16 = 2017. So today's date is February 28, 2017. The question asks: What is the date one year ago from today in MM/DD/YYYY? So one year before February 28, 2017 would be February 28, 2016. But wait, 2016 is a leap year, right? Because 2016 is divisible by 4. So February 2016 had 29 days. But does that affect the calculation? Let me think. If today is February 28, 2017, then going back one year would be February 28, 2016. Even though 2016 is a leap year, the date February 28 still exists. So the answer would be 02/28/2016. Let me check the options. Option C is 02/28/2016. So that's probably the correct answer. Let me make sure I didn't make any mistakes in the dates. Wait, another way to check is to count the years. Jane was born in 2001, so her 16th birthday would be in 2001 + 16 = 2017. Since she was born on February 28, her birthday in 2017 is February 28, 2017. One year before that date is February 28, 2016. So yes, the answer is 02/28/2016, which is option C.
